name: Eggless Pasta Marinara
tagline: Eggless dried pasta with garlicky tomato basil — simple weeknight
highlight: Dried pasta is naturally egg-free; check the label and go

hero.alt: Spaghetti with marinara, basil, and a drizzle of olive oil
hero.caption: marinara — bright, garlicky, simple

healthFacts: Dried pasta in the US is typically egg-free (semolina + water). Check labels for fresh pasta.
Slow-cooked tomato sauce releases more lycopene than raw tomato.
Olive oil added off-heat retains its polyphenols.

steps.title: Bloom the garlic
steps.detail: Warm olive oil in a wide pan over medium-low. Add sliced garlic and chili flakes; cook 3 minutes until just gold.
steps.tip: Burnt garlic ruins the sauce. Pull it the moment it tans.

steps.title: Add tomato
steps.detail: Pour in crushed tomatoes (rinse the can with 1/2 cup water). Salt. Simmer 15 minutes until thickened.
steps.tip: A hand-crushed whole tomato in juice has better flavor than pre-crushed.

steps.title: Boil the pasta
steps.detail: Boil pasta in salted water 1 minute under package time. Reserve 1 cup pasta water; drain.
steps.tip: Pasta water is liquid gold for emulsifying — never skip saving it.

steps.title: Marry pasta and sauce
steps.detail: Tongs pasta into the sauce. Toss with pasta water until silky. Off heat, tear in basil; drizzle olive oil.
steps.tip: Always finish pasta in the sauce, never sauce on top — the pasta has to drink it.

media.alt: Pasta tossed with marinara and basil
media.caption: marry pasta and sauce
